{"query":"Cybercab trademark suspension status Unibev USPTO dispute resolution","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1525,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/02/12/tesla-gets-30-day-extension-to-oppose-cybercab-trademark-held-by-squatter/","title":"Tesla gets 30-day extension to oppose 'Cybercab' trademark held by squatter | Electrek","content":"According to USPTO Trademark Trial and Appeal Board (TTAB) records, Tesla filed the extension request on February 11, 2026, and it was granted the same day.
The entire situation traces back to a fundamental mistake: Tesla unveiled the Cybercab at its “We, Robot” event on October 10, 2024, and CEO Elon Musk name-dropped the product on stage, but the company hadn’t actually filed a trademark application.
Tesla didn’t get its own application in until November 2024.
Meanwhile, the USPTO already rejected Tesla’s attempt to trademark “Robotaxi” in May 2025, deeming it too generic."},{"url":"https://www.teslaoracle.com/2026/02/26/tesla-challenges-unibevs-squatting-of-the-cybercab-trademark-with-the-uspto/","title":"Tesla challenges UniBev's squatting of the 'Cybercab' trademark with the USPTO - Tesla Oracle","content":"On Tuesday, 24th February, the USPTO sent the notice of Tesla’s challenge and trial date to UniBev.
UniBev has 60 days to respond to USPTO’s notice of Tesla’s opposition to the Cybercab trademark.
The automaker plans to start mass production of the vehicle in April 2026.
World's first trillionaire, Elon Musk's space exploration and astronautics company SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) has made a major acquisition within...
Tesla (TSLA) has added Miami, Florida, as one more..."},{"url":"https://secureyourtrademark.com/blog/teslas-cybercab-trademark-suspended/","title":"Why Tesla Can't Use the Cybercab Name It Announced","content":"On October 28, 2024, Unibev submitted its own USPTO application for “Cybercab.” The French company primarily sells hard seltzers, not autonomous vehicles.
Tesla now faces three options: negotiate a purchase from Unibev, challenge the registration through legal proceedings, or rebrand entirely.
When Apple faced an iPad trademark dispute in China, the settlement cost $60 million.
Unibev’s October 28 application would have been the one facing suspension, not Tesla’s.
Since then, he has filed more than 6,000 trademarks with the USPTO."},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/elon-musks-tesla-files-uspto-120109096.html","title":"Elon Musk's Tesla Files USPTO Appeal Against Unibev Over 'Cybercab' Trademark: Report","content":"February 27, 2026 3 min read
Patents and Trademarks Office (USPTO) against French beverage maker Unibev over the "Cybercab" name.
In filings with the USPTO obtained by Electrek on Tuesday, the company has shared a 5-count opposition to Unibev's trademark, accusing the company of fraud, among other things.
The report suggests that Unibev told the USPTO that the "cyber," "cab," or "cyber cab" wasn't being used by anyone for similar goods when filing the trademark, which Tesla argues is false, saying that the EV maker had revealed the name in 2024, but failed to file a trademark in time."},{"url":"https://insideevs.com/news/783634/tesla-cybercab-trademark-suspended-patent/","title":"The Tesla Cybercab Just Got Smacked Down By The Feds—And Not How You'd Expect","content":"The USPTO has suspended Tesla's application until the other company decides to register or drop the Cybercab name claim.
According to a government filing spotted by Electrek, a french beverage company called Unibev filed for the Cybercab trademark several weeks ahead of Tesla, meaning that it's first in line.
"Action on this application is suspended until the prior-filed application(s) below either registers or abandons," the USPTO wrote in a letter to Tesla.
(Tesla eventually abandoned that branding, but because of Mexican regulators not any trademark squatting.)"}]}

A delay in filing paperwork led the United States Patent and Trademark Office to suspending Tesla's application to register the Cybercab trademark ahead of the vehicle's launch. According to a government filing spotted by *Electrek*, a french beverage company called Unibev filed for the Cybercab trademark several weeks ahead of Tesla, meaning that it's first in line. Because Unibev filed for the trademark ahead of Tesla's registration, it takes precedence and, if granted, Tesla's use of Cybercab wouldn't tick at least one of those boxes.","score":0.77199864,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reddit.com/r/teslainvestorsclub/comments/1q5xtkl/teslas_cybercab_trademark_suspended_following","title":"Tesla's Cybercab Trademark Suspended Following Earlier ...","content":"Because Unibev filed first, the USPTO is required to halt Tesla's application until the earlier claim is resolved – either by the company","score":0.74697095,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/elon-musks-tesla-files-uspto-120109096.html","title":"Elon Musk's Tesla Files USPTO Appeal Against Unibev Over 'Cybercab' Trademark: Report","content":"# Elon Musk's Tesla Files USPTO Appeal Against Unibev Over 'Cybercab' Trademark: Report. Billionaire **Elon Musk**-led **Tesla Inc.** (NASDAQ:TSLA) has reportedly filed a fresh appeal with the **U.S. Patents and Trademarks Office** (USPTO) against French beverage maker **Unibev** over the \"Cybercab\" name. In filings with the USPTO obtained by Electrek on Tuesday, the company has shared a 5-count opposition to Unibev's trademark, accusing the company of fraud, among other things. Tesla questioned Unibev's intent to use the trademark as the company argues the wholesaler has no history of manufacturing or selling vehicles and that Unibev's principal, **Jean-Louis Lentali**, follows Tesla officials, including Musk on social media and therefore can't be ignorant about the company's plans for the Cybercab. Tesla accuses Unibev of squatting and said that the brand intended to extract payment from Tesla for the trademark.","score":0.7101667,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/02/12/tesla-gets-30-day-extension-to-oppose-cybercab-trademark-held-by-squatter","title":"Tesla gets 30-day extension to oppose 'Cybercab' ...","content":"Because UNIBEV filed first, the USPTO gave priority to the French company, and Tesla's application was formally suspended as of November 14,","score":0.69697046,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.84,"request_id":"fd82e9d7-ccfd-4742-b72f-28317eed1f38"}
